The party is yet to select nominees for 19 more seats, prominent among them being Chowringhee, Krishnanagar (North) and five constituencies in Murshidabad.There has been intense speculation since last Aug about the RG Kar victim’s mother getting a BJP ticket. She appeared before TV cameras on March 19, saying she wanted to serve as a BJP MLA in the next assembly.She toldon Wednesday, “I appeal to people from all walks of life to support me. A long time has passed since my daughter’s brutal murder, but we are yet to get justice. I have chosen a political platform because I want to fight for justice through the electoral process. Ensuring women’s safety in this state and eradicating corruption in govt hospitals — the very corruption that made my daughter, a brilliant medical student, pay with her life — are my main objectives.Her husband said their election campaign would start on Thursday. “We are receiving overwhelming support. Many have said they will stand by us. Our victory is almost certain. But this is not just about electoral politics, it is about protecting the public interest and preventing another RG Kar-like incident,” he said.However, there was a backlash to her statement, led by activists from the ‘Reclaim the Night’ movement, which had crystallised public revulsion following the horrific crime at RG Kar Medical College and Hospital on Aug 9, 2024.“I am unimaginably hurt that they joined a party which garlands rapists and upholds patriarchy, as a response to what happened in Aug 2024. Bilkis Bano, Kathua, Unnao... it’s heartbreaking to keep count,” said Rimjhim Sinha, a leading member of the ‘Reclaim the Night’ movement.Gender activist and convenor of ‘Reclaim the Night United,’ Satabdi Das, said, “Her decision to contest the polls as a BJP candidate is not at all commendable. We believe it won’t bring justice to any victim, including her daughter.”Left Front has nominated Kalatan Dasgupta, a prominent face of the RG Kar protests, from the same constituency. BJP leadership, however, seems to believe that fielding the victim’s mother would unify anti-Trinamool votes in their favour.“Public outrage swelled over the rape and murder of the doctor. We are preparing our lists and manifesto in consultation with a cross section of society. She came to us and said she wanted to protest the lawlessness in the state and needed a chance to do so. She realised that the BJP is the only force that can dislodge this govt. We admired her fighting spirit and decided to nominate her,” said Bengal BJP president Samik Bhattacharya.Trinamool Congress dubbed the mother’s decision “doublespeak”.“Kolkata Police had arrested the killer within 24 hours. The parents moved court seeking a CBI probe. CBI restated the findings of the police probe. It was they who said CBI was compromised. Who runs CBI? They claimed their requests to meet the PM and the Union home minister, even for a minute, were not granted. So why this change of heart? Why this doublespeak? ” TMC spokesperson Kunal Ghosh said.
Article Summary: The list contained 19 names, bringing the number of candidates picked by the party so far to 275. The party is yet to select nominees for 19 more seats, prominent among them being Chowringhee, Krishnanagar (North) and five constituencies in Murshidabad. There has been intense speculation since last Aug about the RG Kar victim’s mother getting a BJP ticket.

Article Title: Bengaluru Auto Driver News: Post hit-and-run, Bengaluru autorickshaw driver dumps woman’s body in garbage heap
Article Text:
The driver took her to a hospital but, upon realizing she had died, reportedly did the unthinkable—dumped her body in a garbage heap and fled the scene.





BENGALURU: A hit-and-run in Kengeri claimed the life of a 50-year-old woman Monday morning, turning what initially seemed like a mysterious death near a garbage dump into a chilling tale of accident and attempted cover-up. Kempamma, a resident of Vinayakanagar in Kengeri Satellite Town, sustained severe head injuries after being struck by an autorickshaw.The driver took her to a hospital but, upon realizing she had died, reportedly did the unthinkable—dumped her body in a garbage heap and fled the scene.Kengeri police have arrested 50-year-old autorickshaw driver Basavegowda, a resident of Laggere, following CCTV analysis and eyewitness accounts.The vehicle involved, bearing registration KA-02-AG-4616, hit Kempamma near a Domino's Pizza outlet while Basavegowda was ferrying two passengers to Kengeri railway station. Eyewitnesses immediately called the 108 ambulance service, but help did not arrive in time.Basavegowda later confessed that Kempamma suddenly walked onto the road and, unable to control the vehicle, he struck her, leaving her critically injured.The passengers, reportedly in a rush to catch their train, left the scene shortly after the accident. With assistance from nearby restaurant employees, Basavegowda placed the injured woman in his autorickshaw and rushed her to a private hospital, where staff allegedly refused treatment due to lack of facilities. He then drove her to Rajarajeshwari Hospital, but upon checking her condition at the hospital gate, he realized she had died.Panicked, Basavegowda allegedly transported the body to a garbage dump near the BDA apartment and abandoned it, in an attempt to evade responsibility.Police arrested him Tuesday night and took him into custody to reconstruct the sequence of events and corroborate evidence. "We have taken him into police custody to reconstruct the crime and match the chain of events with evidence. The case will later be handed over to traffic police," said deputy commissioner of police (South-West) Anitha B Haddannavar.A senior officer added that efforts are under way to trace the two passengers who had booked the auto through a taxi aggregator, as their statements are crucial to the investigation. "We have collected CCTV footage from multiple locations and are convinced this is an accident, not a murder," the officer said.
Article Summary: Kengeri police have arrested 50-year-old autorickshaw driver Basavegowda, following CCTV analysis and eyewitness accounts. Kempamma, a resident of Vinayakanagar in Kengeri Satellite Town, sustained severe head injuries after being struck by an auto. The driver took her to a hospital but, upon realizing she had died, reportedly did the unthinkable—dumped her body in a garbage heap.

Article Title: Elon Musk's TSA offer that Donald Trump loved has been rejected due to ...
Article Text:
Funding crisis due to US partial shutdown

Watch Trump’s SHOCK Update on Elon Musk; Reveals PRIVATE Details In Front Of Cabinet | 'BIG BEAUTIFUL...' Trump’s SHOCK Update on Elon Musk; Reveals PRIVATE Details In Front Of Cabinet | 'BIG BEAUTIFUL...'

The White House has rejected Elon Musk’s offer to fund Transportation Security Administration (TSA) workers’ salaries. Last week, Musk shared an X post writing “I would like to offer to pay the salaries of TSA personnel during this funding impasse that is negatively affecting the lives of so many Americans at airports throughout the country. ” Rejecting the offer, Abigail Jackson, a White House spokesperson told Fortune: “We greatly appreciate Elon’s generous offer. This would pose great legal challenges due to his involvement with federal government contracts.” He further added that the fastest way to ensure TSA employees get paid would be for “Democrats to fund the Department of Homeland Security.”TSA that comes under the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) is currently facing a funding issue as Congress debates its immigration policies.Because of the partial shutdown that began on January 30, TSA agents received their first zero pay cheque on March 13.According to the DHS, up to 10% of TSA agents did not report to work on some days this week. In some airports, absence rates have reached around 20%. TSA agents usually start with a salary of about $40,000 a year, and the DHS employs nearly 50,000 of them.Due to the shutdown, the passengers are experiencing longer waits and unpredictable checkpoint staffing.On March 25, TSA said that the wait times have hit the worst levels in the agency’s history, with some passengers waiting more than four and a half hours to clear security.As quoted in a Fortune report, acting Administrator Ha Nguyen McNeill told a House Homeland Security Committee hearing that TSA has lost more than 480 transportation security officers since the funding lapse began on Feb. 14, now roughly 40 days ago.
Article Summary: The White House has rejected Elon Musk’s offer to fund Transportation Security Administration (TSA) workers’ salaries. Musk shared an X post writing “I would like to offer to pay the salaries of TSA personnel during this funding impasse that is negatively affecting the lives of so many Americans at airports throughout the country. ” TSA that comes under the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) is currently facing a funding issue as Congress debates its immigration policies.

Article Title: Saudi Arabia latest visa updates for stranded tourists: Fine-free exit, new extension rules amid Iran vs US-Israel war
Article Text:
Saudi Arabia grants visa exemption and fine free exit of stranded tourist/Representative Image

Watch 'MBS Is Fighting With Us': Trump Puts Stamp On Saudi Arabia's Role In Raging Iran War | Watch 'MBS Is Fighting With Us': Trump Puts Stamp On Saudi Arabia's Role In Raging Iran War | Watch





Saudi Arabia has announced temporary measures to address the status of visitors whose visas have expired, offering flexibility to those unable to leave due to disruptions caused by the ongoing US-Israel war with Iran. The move comes as authorities respond to travel difficulties linked to the conflict.The Ministry of Interior said the decision applies to holders of visit visas, Umrah visas, transit visas and final exit permits that expired around late February 2026. The initiative is aimed at helping those who were unable to depart the Kingdom as planned.Under the new rules, affected individuals have two options. They can choose to extend their expired visas, or leave the country directly without facing penalties.In the first option, visa holders can apply for an extension through their host. The extension will be valid until April 18, 2026, provided the required fees are paid through the Absher platform. This allows visitors to regularise their stay and remain in the Kingdom for a limited period.Alternatively, individuals whose visas have expired will be allowed to exit Saudi Arabia directly through international departure points.In this case, there will be no requirement to renew the visa, and no fees or overstay fines will be imposed. This option is intended to ease the departure process for those looking to leave immediately.The ministry clarified that the measures cover visas that expired on February 25 and 26, 2026, ensuring that all affected individuals during this period are included under the directive.Authorities have urged all eligible visa holders to take advantage of this grace period and complete their departure before April 18. Failure to do so could result in penalties under Saudi regulations.The move, implemented under directives from the Saudi leadership, is designed to facilitate procedures for visitors and residents affected by the current regional conditions. Officials said the decision also aims to maintain smooth travel operations across international entry and exit points in the Kingdom while ensuring compliance with existing rules.
Article Summary: Saudi Arabia has announced temporary measures to address the status of visitors whose visas have expired. The move comes as authorities respond to travel difficulties linked to the conflict. The initiative is aimed at helping those who were unable to depart the Kingdom as planned. Officials said the decision also aims to maintain smooth travel operations across international entry and exit points.

Article Title: Sarah R Jay: Meet Sarah R Jay: UAE singer launches app to solve emirates' escalating pet abandonment crisis amid Iran vs US-Israel war
Article Text:
Meet the UAE Singer Who Turned Compassion Into a Lifeline for Stray Animals In The UAE Amid Iran vs US-Israel War

A silent pet crisis unfolding across the UAE



Sarah R Jay's StraySpotter Offers Solution to UAE's Pet Crisis Fuelled by Iran vs US-Israel War

One artist’s response: Sarah R Jay turning pet empathy into UAE infrastructure

UAE Pet Crisis: Artist Sarah R Jay Launches StraySpotter Platform Amid Iran vs US-Israel War

UAE pet crisis shaped by Iran vs US-Israel war



A new kind of leadership in the UAE by Sarah R Jay amid Iran vs US-Israel war







In a week dominated by headlines of abandoned pets, overwhelmed shelters and a deepening humanitarian concern for animals in the UAE amid Iran vs US-Israel war, one unexpected figure has stepped forward, not from the world of policy or rescue operations but from music. Meet Sarah R Jay, a Ras Al Khaimah-based artist who has transformed concern into action, launching a digital lifeline for animals caught in the crossfire of crisis.Over the past few weeks, the UAE has witnessed a troubling surge in pet abandonment, as regional instability and sudden relocations force many expatriates to leave, sometimes without their pet animals. Animal shelters and rescue groups have reported being overwhelmed with new cases, under-resourced and stretched thin and flooded with emergency calls.Global reports describe heartbreaking scenes of pets left in empty apartments, tied to poles or surrendered in desperation as families rush to exit the country. What was once a manageable issue has rapidly escalated into a full-blown animal welfare crisis, with thousands of animals suddenly displaced and dependent on already strained networks.While many watched the crisis unfold online, Sarah R Jay chose a different path and built a solution.Recognising a critical gap between people who spot abandoned animals and those who can help, she launched StraySpotter.com, a digital platform designed to streamline rescue efforts.The idea is deceptively simple where users can report abandoned or injured animals, upload photos and exact locations and mark urgency levels to prioritise rescue. In a landscape where time often determines survival, this kind of real-time, crowd-powered reporting system could be transformative.She noted that there was a huge gap and pointed out that rescue organisations are already overwhelmed and need faster, more efficient ways to respond. What makes this initiative stand out is not just its purpose but its timing. At a moment when pet shelters are operating at capacity, volunteers are stretched to their limits and cases are rising faster than response times, StraySpotter introduces something urgently needed and that is coordination.Rather than replacing existing rescue networks, the platform acts as a bridge, connecting everyday residents with animal welfare volunteers and rescue organisations. In effect, it transforms passive concern into actionable data.The surge in abandoned pets is not happening in isolation. It is deeply tied to broader geopolitical tensions, including the recent regional conflict that has triggered mass departures from the UAE. As families leave quickly, pet relocation becomes complex and expensive, documentation and logistics create barriers and some animals are tragically left behind.Experts and activists warn that pets are becoming the “hidden victims” of crisis, entirely dependent on human decisions they cannot control. The crisis has sparked widespread outrage and grief online. Images and stories of abandoned animals have gone viral, prompting calls for stricter enforcement of animal welfare laws, appeals for responsible pet ownership and increased donations and volunteer efforts.In the UAE, abandoning pets is illegal and can result in heavy fines or penalties, yet enforcement alone cannot solve what is fundamentally a human behaviour issue under pressure.What Sarah R Jay’s initiative represents is a broader shift, one where individuals outside traditional systems step in to address urgent societal gaps. She is not a policymaker neither is she running a shelter. Yet, by identifying a system failure, the lack of connection between sightings and rescues, she has created a tool that could scale rapidly across communities.It is a reminder that in the digital age, impact does not always come from institutions. Sometimes, it begins with a single idea and the willingness to act on it. The crisis has also forced a deeper conversation about ethical pet ownership, emergency preparedness for expatriates and the role of community in times of disruption.Advocates are increasingly calling for better relocation support systems, centralised rescue coordination and public awareness campaigns because ultimately, technology alone cannot fix the problem but it can amplify solutions. In a country known for its innovation and rapid response to challenges, the emergence of platforms like StraySpotter reflects a growing intersection of technology, community action and compassion-driven problem-solving.As the UAE navigates both environmental and geopolitical uncertainties and at a time when abandoned pets are becoming one of the most heartbreaking consequences of crisis, Sarah R Jay has turned awareness into action. Her platform rescues animals, empowers people, connects urgency with response and most importantly, it reminds us that even in moments of chaos, compassion can be organised and scaled.
Article Summary: Artist Sarah R Jay launched StraySpotter.com, a digital platform designed to streamline rescue efforts. The platform acts as a bridge, connecting everyday residents with animal welfare volunteers and rescue organisations. The surge in abandoned pets is deeply tied to broader geopolitical tensions, including the recent regional conflict.

Article Title: Australia Iran Travel Ban: Australia announces travel ban on Iranians for 6 months amid Middle East tensions
Article Text:
Why was the ban imposed

Who will be affected

Up to 7,200 Iranian nationals holding valid tourist visas

Only those currently outside Australia

Specifically applies to Visitor (Subclass 600) visa holders

The restriction does not cancel visas, but temporarily prevents entry into the country.

Who is exempted

Certain categories will not be impacted by the ban:

Iranian nationals already in Australia or in transit

Spouses and dependent children of Australian citizens or permanent residents

Individuals granted special clearance through a “permitted travel certificate”

Cases involving Iranian parents of Australian citizens, who may receive “sympathetic consideration”

Special permissions possible

Temporary measure, subject to review





Australia has announced a temporary ban on Iranian tourists, citing concerns over regional instability and the risk that visitors may not be able to return home.The move, announced by Home Affairs Minister Tony Burke, will prevent entry of Iranian nationals holding tourist visas for an initial period of six months.Officials said the decision is aimed at “protecting the integrity and sustainability” of the country’s migration system amid rapidly evolving global conditions.The Australian government said the restrictions were necessary due to the ongoing conflict and uncertainty involving Iran and the broader Middle East.Authorities fear that some visitors entering on temporary visas may be “unable or unlikely” to leave Australia once their visas expire.“There are many visitor visas issued before the conflict that may not have been granted under current conditions,” Burke said, adding that migration outcomes should be “deliberate decisions,” not accidental results of travel disruptions.The government indicated that a limited number of travellers may still be allowed entry on a case-by-case basis.Applicants can seek exemptions through special travel permissions, particularly under compelling or humanitarian circumstances.The restriction will remain in place for six months, after which the government must reassess and decide whether to extend it.Officials emphasised that the situation is being closely monitored and policies may be adjusted depending on developments in the region.The decision has drawn criticism from refugee and humanitarian groups, who argue it unfairly targets individuals who followed visa rules and made travel plans in good faith.Critics say the move risks undermining confidence in Australia’s migration system and adds stress to communities already affected by the crisis in Iran.
Article Summary: Up to 7,200 Iranian nationals holding valid tourist visas will be affected. Restriction does not cancel visas, but temporarily prevents entry into the country. Home Affairs Minister Tony Burke said the decision is aimed at ‘protecting the integrity and sustainability’ of the country’s migration system.

Article Title: Washington Dc Shooting: 2 arrested after US Park Police officer shot in Washington DC while on duty; targeted attack suspected
Article Text:
Suspects held within 24 hours

Officer shot while in unmarked vehicle

Condition stable; officer discharged

Crime scene evidence and local fear

Search operations and wider probe

Background details emerge





Washington, DC Metropolitan Police have arrested two suspects in connection with the shooting of a US Park Police officer in southeast Washington on Monday evening, the Associated Press reported citing officials.Authorities believe the officer may have been deliberately targeted, though a motive has not yet been established.The Metropolitan Police Department (MPD) identified the accused as Darren Foster (21) and Asheile Foster (22), both residents of southeast DC Darren was arrested shortly after the incident, while Asheile was taken into custody the following day.Both have been charged with assault on a federal police officer with a firearm.The incident took place around 7:30 pm when the officer, working on an investigation within US Park Police jurisdiction, was inside an unmarked vehicle.Two armed men allegedly approached and opened fire, striking the officer in the upper body, reportedly in the shoulder.Despite being injured, the officer managed to drive away from the scene before being airlifted to a hospital by the Park Police aviation unit.Officials confirmed that the injuries were not life-threatening.The officer, described as a veteran, has since been discharged from the hospital.Investigators indicated that the attackers may have known the individual inside the vehicle was a police officer. Interim police leadership noted that the shooting appeared to be a targeted act, though no further details on intent have been released.Visuals from the scene showed a bullet-riddled vehicle and multiple shell casings scattered across the area. Residents reported hearing rapid gunfire, describing the incident as “very serious” and frightening, raising concerns about neighborhood safety.Police executed a search warrant at a residence near the shooting site on Tuesday, with K-9 units assisting in the operation. Investigators were seen removing items from the property.The case is being handled by MPD’s NIBIN Investigations Unit, with support from federal agencies, including the Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms and Explosives, along with US Park Police detectives.Court records indicate that Asheile Foster has faced prior charges, including motor vehicle theft and weapons violations. Reports also suggest he was released following a court hearing just hours before the shooting, though officials have not formally linked that development to the incident.Authorities continue to examine evidence and have requested anyone with information to come forward as the probe into the shooting intensifies.
Article Summary: Officer shot while in unmarked vehicle in southeast Washington, DC. Suspects identified as Darren Foster (21) and Asheile Foster (22) Both have been charged with assault on a federal police officer with a firearm. Officers believe the officer may have been deliberately targeted.

Article Title: London Arson Attack: UK police arrest 2 men in antisemitic arson attack on Jewish community ambulances in London
Article Text:
Attack in Golders Green sparks fear

Hate crime angle; international links probed

Security stepped up





Two men have been arrested in connection with a suspected antisemitic arson attack in which four ambulances belonging to Jewish charity Hatzola were set ablaze in north London.The suspects, aged 45 and 47, were detained by the Metropolitan Police on suspicion of arson with intent to endanger life.Authorities described the arrests as a “significant breakthrough” but cautioned that investigations remain ongoing, with CCTV footage indicating at least three individuals may have been involved.The incident occurred in Golders Green, an area with a sizable Jewish population, in the early hours of Monday. Explosions triggered by oxygen and gas canisters inside the ambulances shattered nearby windows and forced some residents to evacuate.While no injuries were reported, three ambulances were completely destroyed and another severely damaged, raising alarm within the local community already concerned about rising antisemitism.UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer condemned the incident, calling it a “deeply shocking antisemitic arson attack” and asserting that “antisemitism has no place in our society” as authorities continue their investigation.Police are treating the case as an antisemitic hate crime, though it has not been formally classified as terrorism. The probe is being led by counter-terrorism officers due to the seriousness of the incident.Investigators are examining a claim of responsibility by a little-known group, Harakat Ashab al-Yamin al-Islamiya, which has alleged links to pro-Iran networks. UK authorities have previously warned of Iran-backed plots targeting Jewish communities and dissidents across Europe.Additional security measures, including increased armed patrols, have been deployed in the area as a precaution. The Community Security Trust said it welcomed the arrests but warned that concerns within the community remain high.Meanwhile, more than £1.3 million has been raised through public donations to support the charity, which provides free emergency medical services regardless of faith.
Article Summary: Two men arrested in connection with suspected antisemitic arson attack. Four ambulances belonging to Jewish charity Hatzola were set ablaze in north London. No injuries were reported, but three ambulances were completely destroyed and another severely damaged. UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer condemned the incident, calling it a “deeply shocking’ attack.

Article Title: US-Iran mediation: JD Vance might visit Pakistan over weekend to talk it out with Tehran
Article Text:
Watch 'NOBODY LIKES WAR': JD Vance Shocks Trump On Iran; Breaks Silence On America's 'ENDLESS WARS' 'NOBODY LIKES WAR': JD Vance Shocks Trump On Iran; Breaks Silence On America's 'ENDLESS WARS'





Amid ongoing efforts to de-escalate the Middle East conflict, US vice president JD Vance may travel to Pakistan later this week for talks aimed at finding an off-ramp to the war with Iran, according to a CNN report citing administration officials.The proposed visit comes as the Trump administration explores backchannel diplomacy with Tehran, with Pakistan and Turkey emerging as possible venues for discussions. Officials told CNN that preparations are underway for a potential meeting involving Vance and other senior US representatives, though the timing, location and participants remain fluid.The development follows indications that Iranian representatives are reluctant to engage with US envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner, and may prefer to negotiate through Vance.US President Donald Trump said earlier that Vance, secretary of state Marco Rubio, Kushner and Witkoff are all involved in the ongoing diplomatic efforts.However, the White House has downplayed suggestions that Vance’s role marks any shift in strategy. Press secretary Karoline Leavitt said, “I don’t think anything has changed. The vice president has always been a key member, the president’s right-hand man and a key member of the president’s national security team.He’s been part of these discussions throughout this entire course of the administration.”She added that President Trump seeks Vance’s counsel “on all matters, both foreign and domestic.”Leavitt declined to disclose details about the Iranian counterparts involved in the talks, saying, “We’re not gonna get into the details of these negotiations and conversations that continue to take place. As, of course, you can imagine, they are very sensitive diplomatic discussions.”She reiterated that discussions are ongoing even as the US maintains military pressure, warning that if “Iran fails to accept the reality of the current moment,” Trump “is prepared to unleash hell.”Pakistan has signalled its willingness to facilitate talks. Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif has said Islamabad is ready to support “meaningful and conclusive talks,” with reports suggesting the country is positioning itself as a venue for negotiations. A Guardian report cited Pakistani officials as saying a meeting could take place as early as this week.At the same time, regional actors have urged caution. According to CNN, Gulf allies have privately warned the US against escalating the conflict further, particularly against any move to deploy ground troops or seize strategic locations such as Kharg Island, citing risks of wider retaliation and prolonged instability.The diplomatic push comes alongside continued military buildup, with reports that around 1,000 US troops from the 82nd Airborne Division could be deployed to the region. With both diplomatic and military tracks advancing simultaneously, the prospects for talks remain uncertain as the conflict continues unabated into its fourth week.
Article Summary: US vice president JD Vance may travel to Pakistan later this week for talks aimed at finding an off-ramp to the war with Iran, according to a CNN report. The proposed visit comes as the Trump administration explores backchannel diplomacy with Tehran, with Pakistan and Turkey emerging as possible venues.

Article Title: 'Can’t tell him every single thing': Report claims Trump shown video daily of 'successful' strikes on Iran
Article Text:
White House photo

Watch Trump Seeks Ceasefire As Rare Earth Stocks Dwindle Threatening Weapons Supply, ‘Only Two Months…’ Trump Seeks Ceasefire As Rare Earth Stocks Dwindle Threatening Weapons Supply, ‘Only Two Months…’

10,000 targets hit: US





United States said on Wednesday it had crossed 10,000 strikes and its ongoing military campaign was “on plan or ahead of plan”, but a report suggested that what reached Donald Trump may be only part of the picture.NBC News, citing an official, said US military have been compiling daily video updates for Trump since the start of the war, showing only the “biggest, most successful” strikes from the previous 48 hours. These videos, however, did not reflect the full scope of the conflict.“We can’t tell him every single thing that happens,” the official reportedly said, adding that briefings tend to get better responses when they focus on US successes.White House secretary, Karoline Leavitt, rejected the notion, saying, “That’s an absolutely false assertion coming from someone who has not been present in the room,” Leavitt said in a statement.In a video update on Wednesday, US Central Command chief Brad Cooper said the operation, now in its fourth week, had overwhelmed Iran’s air defence systems and was having “tangible effects”."We hit the 10,000th Iranian target just hours ago. And if you combine what we've accomplished with the success of our Israeli ally, together we have struck thousands more, clearly demonstrating that we're stronger together.Our precision strikes have overwhelmed Iranian air defences and our combat flights over Iran are having tangible effects.He said 92 per cent of Iran’s largest warships were destroyed. “They’re not sailing,” he said, adding that Iran had lost the ability to “meaningfully project naval power.”"For decades, Iranian warships have roamed regional waters, threatening and harassing global shipping. But those days are over because we've now destroyed 92 percent of the Iranian Navy's largest vessels. They're not sailing, and my operational assessment is that they've now lost the ability to meaningfully project naval power and influence around the region and around the world," he said.Cooper also said missile and drone launch rates had dropped by more than 90 per cent, and that more than two-thirds of Iran’s missile, drone, and naval production facilities were damaged or destroyed."Iran's drone and missile launch rates are down by more than 90 percent, meaning their ability to attack American forces and regional neighbors is drastically reduced. And not only have we significantly degraded Iran's naval drone and missile capabilities, we've also removed the regime's ability to rebuild them," he said.
Article Summary: US Central Command chief Brad Cooper said the operation, now in its fourth week, had overwhelmed Iran’s air defence systems and was having “tangible effects” He said 92 per cent of Iran's largest warships were destroyed.

Article Title: ‘Disgraceful’: Karoline Leavitt slams Larry Krasner over arrest threat to ICE agents deployed at US airports
Article Text:
‘Agents are helping, not breaking the law’

Watch 'AMERICA WILL UNLEASH HELL': Karoline Leavitt's Message To Mojtaba Khamenei Over Trump's Iran Offer 'AMERICA WILL UNLEASH HELL': Karoline Leavitt's Message To Mojtaba Khamenei Over Trump's Iran Offer

Strong defence of ICE personnel

What Krasner said about ICE





White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt on Wednesday hit back at Philadelphia District Attorney Larry Krasner after he warned that immigration agents could face arrest, calling his remarks “disgraceful. ”Responding during a press briefing, the White House press secretary questioned the basis of Krasner’s comments and criticised the tone of his warning.The White House press secretary pointed out that personnel from US Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) were assisting travellers at airports rather than engaging in unlawful activity.“He’s talking to the ICE agents who are handing out water bottles and helping people move through lines at airports,” she said, dismissing the suggestion that agents were committing crimes.Defending the agency, the White House press secretary described ICE officers as “great people” carrying out critical responsibilities.She added that their work extends beyond airports to maintaining public safety nationwide, including tackling serious criminal threats.The White House press secretary also took aim at Krasner’s stance, suggesting he should speak directly with ICE personnel to understand their role instead of issuing threats.Her remarks underscored the administration’s firm defence of federal agents amid criticism from local officials.Earlier, Krasner had warned that federal immigration agents operating in Philadelphia could face legal consequences if they violated local laws.He asserted that any individual committing offences within the city’s jurisdiction would be prosecuted, regardless of federal authority.Krasner also said that presidential pardons would not shield individuals from local prosecution and stressed that his office would take action if laws were broken, while maintaining that his role was to uphold the Constitution and protect public safety.
Article Summary: The White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt hit back at Philadelphia District Attorney Larry Krasner after he warned that immigration agents could face arrest. She said that personnel from US Immigration and Customs Enforcement were assisting travellers at airports rather than engaging in unlawful activity. Her remarks underscored the administration's firm defence of federal agents amid criticism from local officials.

Article Title: Midtown East Hit-And-Run: NYC horror: 69-year-old woman killed in hit-and-run, SUV driver on the run
Article Text:
Victim rushed to hospital, succumbed to injuries

Back-to-back fatal crashes raise concern





A 69-year-old woman was fatally struck by a speeding SUV in a shocking hit-and-run incident in Midtown East during the early hours of Wednesday, police said.The victim was hit around 4:30 a.m. at the intersection of East 57th Street and Second Avenue, an area that has witnessed multiple recent crashes.According to authorities, the impact was so severe that the woman was thrown off her feet, with both her shoes flung across the street.Surveillance footage from the dimly lit roadway shows the black SUV barreling through the stretch before striking the pedestrian and fleeing the scene without stopping.The woman was immediately taken to New York-Presbyterian/Weill Cornell Medical Center, where she later died from her injuries.Police have not yet released her identity as they are in the process of notifying her family.Video evidence suggests the driver slowed briefly after the collision but did not stop, turning onto East 57th Street and escaping. Meanwhile, a second SUV stopped at the scene, and two individuals stepped out to assist the victim.The incident marks the second deadly hit-and-run in the neighbourhood within a week. Just days earlier, a 44-year-old man was killed nearby when a truck driver reversing into a crosswalk struck him.That driver was later arrested.According to the residents, the repeated accidents, particularly during early morning hours, are deeply unsettling. Police are now examining footage from multiple cameras in the area, hoping to identify the fleeing SUV and bring the driver to justice.
Article Summary: The 69-year-old woman was fatally struck by a speeding SUV in a shocking hit- and-run incident in Midtown East during the early hours of Wednesday. The impact was so severe that the woman was thrown off her feet, with both her shoes flung across the street. The woman was immediately taken to New York- Presbyterian/Weill Cornell Medical Center, where she later died from her injuries.

Article Title: Zohran Mamdani: Zohran Mamdani gets heckled during a press conference, says 'If I don't hear him yelling at me...'
Article Text:
NYC mayor Zohran Mamdani gets heckled at a press conference. He chose to address the heckler.



Watch NYC Mayor Mamdani ‘Heckled’ Outside Gracie Mansion As Epstein Records Mention His Mother NYC Mayor Mamdani ‘Heckled’ Outside Gracie Mansion As Epstein Records Mention His Mother





New York City mayor Zohran Mamdani was interrupted by a heckler as he was addressing a press conference on affordable housing. As Mamdani was speaking, he segued to the heckler and said in NYC, all voices will be heard. "I want that man to be able to afford to keep living in NYC. If I don't hear him yelling at me. If I don't hear him yelling at me, it means he got priced out of this place. I don't want that. I don't want that for him. I don't want that for anybody in this city. We need to make this city affordable enough to yell at your politicians. If it's not the city, I don't want to live in that city," Mamdani said as the man continued shouting in the background.Mamdani announced the Neighborhood Builders Fast Track, which he says will create a prequalified roster of developers. It will cut down on pre-development time for new projects from 18 months to 10 months, he said.The heckler started shouting when Mamdani asked during the press conference how many people present there had been crushed by rent year after year. "Who here has considered leaving this city because of the expenses it takes to call yourself a New Yorker? Who here has looked around the block and wondered where my neighbors have gone?" Mamdani said.The man continued shouting as Mamdani chose to address the shouting."Mamdani has the unique skill of knowing how to handle and walk into every room and engage with people, no matter how toxic they are or how their feelings are against him. And many times, make them feel heard," David Schwartz, former executive committee member of Brooklyn's Democratic Party said, sharing the video."Elected officials, nonprofit and religious leaders, advocates, and activists on both sides of the political spectrum who met with him told me that. Don't take my word for it, President Trump is pretty public about it. That's why he's currently the mayor of New York City. If you don't like his politics or you're like me, who opposed his candidacy and is still troubled by some of his actions and statements, then don't take this post the wrong way; read into it how you should be better to communicate your message," the post praising Mamdani read.
Article Summary: Mamdani announced the Neighborhood Builders Fast Track, which he says will create a prequalified roster of developers. The heckler started shouting when Mamdani asked how many people present there had been crushed by rent year after year. He chose to address the heckler.

Article Title: 'I may call up the national guard': Trump rants amid Iran tensions; blames Democrats for immigration, airport chaos
Article Text:
Watch US Military Targeted In Iraq As Proxy War Explodes, Missiles Hit Key Bases | Watch US Military Targeted In Iraq As Proxy War Explodes, Missiles Hit Key Bases | Watch





US President Donald Trump on Wednesday accused Democrats of attempting to “create inner chaos” to distract from what he described as a “great military achievement” in Iran, while also escalating his rhetoric on immigration and signaling he may deploy the National Guard to address issues at airports.In a series of posts on Truth Social, Trump alleged that political opponents were undermining national interests and border enforcement efforts, even as he praised immigration authorities and hinted at stronger federal action.“The Radical Left, Country-Hating Democrats are trying to create inner chaos to take away from the great military achievement we are having in Iran. They hate seeing us WIN so completely and decisively, but the American public sees what is going on.AMERICA FIRST! President DJT,” Trump wrote.The remarks come amid heightened tensions in the Middle East and ongoing domestic debates over immigration policy and border security. Trump also claimed Democrats were obstructing legislative agreements unless provisions for amnesty and citizenship were included.“The Democrats don’t want to make any deal unless Amnesty and citizenship are given to millions of Criminals who have entered our Country illegally, many of whom have been convicted of serious crimes.PROTECT AMERICA! President DJT,” he said.Turning to domestic infrastructure concerns, Trump blamed Democrats for issues at U.S. airports, accusing them of prioritizing undocumented immigrants over American citizens. He suggested that federal intervention could be expanded if necessary.“Blame the Democrats for the Airport’s mess. They want our Country to do badly. They want our Country to fail. They broke the already signed Bill because they want to take care of Criminals who enter our Country illegally, rather than American citizens. Thank you to our great ICE Patriots for helping. It makes a big difference. I may call up the National Guard for more help. President DJT.”The posts reflect Trump’s continued focus on immigration enforcement, national security, and partisan criticism as key themes in his public messaging, while underscoring the sharp political divide over border policy and federal authority.
Article Summary: US President Donald Trump accused Democrats of attempting to “create inner chaos” to distract from what he described as a “great military achievement” in Iran. Trump also escalating his rhetoric on immigration and signaling he may deploy the National Guard to address issues at airports.

Article Title: Pakistan train derailment: 25 injured as Tezgam Express coaches go off track
Article Text:
The derailed train (Picture source: X)





At least 25 people were injured after seven coaches of a train derailed in Pakistan’s Punjab province late on Tuesday night. The incident took place in Lodhran, around 400 km from Lahore, forcing authorities to suspend rail traffic on the affected track.The Karachi-bound Tezgam Express had left Lahore Railway Station in the evening and derailed near Adam Wahan Railway Station on the Bahawalpur-Lodhran section at around 9.30 pm.“The train derailed when it reached near Adam Wahan Railway Station. Seven bogies went off the track, with some passengers trapped inside," officials said as quoted by news agency PTI.“Some 25 passengers have been injured, one of them seriously.However, no casualty has been reported,” they added.Rescue teams from Punjab Emergency Department Rescue 1122 reached the spot, pulled out trapped passengers and shifted the injured to a nearby hospital.Federal Minister for Railways Hanif Abbasi said that human error and possible mechanical issues were likely behind the derailment.A railway official said the incident may have been caused by couplers between the bogies breaking, which split the train into two parts. The coaches attached to the engine derailed after the separation, he added.Railway traffic on the route remains suspended as authorities continue inspection and restoration work.
Article Summary: The Karachi-bound Tezgam Express had left Lahore Railway Station in the evening and derailed near Adam Wahan Railway Station. Seven bogies went off the track, with some passengers trapped inside. The incident took place in Lodhran, around 400 km from Lahore.
